author: y4rr
content: Plan: XpuDevice::is_available() currently returns cfg!(feature = 'wgpu') — a compile-time check only. torch.xpu.is_available() probes for actual hardware and returns False on machines with no Intel GPU. The fix: under the wgpu feature, use catch_unwind around CubeRuntime::new(CubeDevice::Wgpu(0)) to probe for an actual adapter at runtime. If new() succeeds, return true; if it panics or errors, return false. This matches the existing xpu() helper pattern used in lib.rs unit tests, and requires no new dependencies (CubeRuntime and CubeDevice are already imported under cfg(feature='wgpu')). Conformance: remove cascade_skip in xpu_is_available_matches_fixture and xpu_device_new_errors_without_backend once the assertion holds. §3 phrasing: Fixed XpuDevice::is_available() to probe wgpu adapter list for Intel GPU instead of assuming feature-compile-time means available.
